text_long: Voice dictation has quietly become one of the most important productivity tools for software developers and writers alike. Instead of typing every sentence by hand, you can simply speak your thoughts out loud and watch them appear on the screen almost instantly. The best dictation systems handle punctuation, capitalization and even technical jargon like Kubernetes, GitHub repositories, and asynchronous JavaScript functions without missing a beat. However, Most commercial offerings send your audio to remote servers for processing, which raises serious privacy questions for anyone working with confidential information. A fully local solution processes everything on your own machine, so your words never leave your laptop. The challenge, of course, is running these large neural networks fast enough on ordinary consumer hardware, without a dedicated graphics card, while still keeping the transcription accurate and responsive.
text_short: Send a message to the team that the demo is ready for review tomorrow morning.
